datasets.title: EPA Air Quality System (AQS)
datasets.subtitle: Ambient air pollution monitoring network across the United States
datasets.notes:
## Overview
The EPA AQS dataset contains measurements from over 4,000 monitoring stations across the US, Puerto Rico, and the Virgin Islands. Data includes criteria pollutants (PM2.5, PM10, O₃, CO, SO₂, NO₂) and air toxics.

## Key Observations
- Excellent temporal depth going back to the 1980s
- Station density varies significantly by region
- Requires careful handling of data gaps and instrument changes

## Preprocessing Notes
Use the pre-generated annual summary files for faster loading. The raw hourly files are very large (~5 GB/year for PM2.5 alone).

datasets.title: OpenAQ Global Air Quality
datasets.subtitle: Open-source aggregator of real-time air quality data worldwide
datasets.notes:
## Overview
OpenAQ aggregates data from government air quality monitoring networks worldwide. Free API with generous rate limits.

## Coverage Notes
Coverage is uneven — very dense in Europe and North America, sparse in Africa and parts of Asia.

## Data Quality
Variable: data comes directly from national agencies with no harmonization. Units and pollutant definitions can differ across countries.

datasets.title: USGS National Water Quality Monitoring Council
datasets.subtitle: Integrated federal water quality data across US watersheds
datasets.notes:
## Overview
Comprehensive multi-agency dataset combining USGS, EPA, and state agency water quality measurements.

## Parameters
Physical (temp, turbidity, flow), chemical (nutrients, metals, organics), and biological indicators.

## Access
Excellent REST API via the Water Quality Portal. Can filter by state, HUC, parameter, date range.

datasets.title: Global Forest Watch — Tree Cover Loss
datasets.subtitle: Annual global tree cover loss at 30m resolution from Hansen et al.
datasets.notes:
## Overview
The gold-standard dataset for global deforestation monitoring. Uses Landsat imagery to track annual tree cover gain and loss.

## Key Caveats
- "Tree cover loss" ≠ deforestation: includes plantation harvests, fire, windthrow
- Definition: ≥30% canopy cover threshold
- Small patches (<0.09 ha) may be missed

## Recommended Usage
Combine with land-use data (e.g., PRODES for Brazil, Global Land Cover) to distinguish permanent conversion from temporary disturbance.
